plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs to eliminate that risk.
Trim QQQI and SPY to reduce concentration and free up cash.
Buy CRM and CEG as strong, high-scoring new positions.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with lower leverage.
Expect a lower monthly target with reduced potential for large drawdowns.
conservative.read: This plan targets around 1.5% per month with a strong focus on capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration risk, holding a larger cash buffer. Expect lower potential drawdowns but also a more modest growth outlook.

plan: Trim SOXL and sell SPAL to reduce leverage.
Trim SPY and QQQI to lower concentration.
Add META and NVDU as strong, high-scoring new positions.
This plan balances growth with risk reduction.
It aims for steady growth with moderate drawdowns.
balanced.read: This plan targets around 2.5% per month by reducing leverage and concentration while adding quality names. It balances broad index exposure with growth-oriented tech. Drawdowns are expected to be moderate, aligning with a risk-first growth approach.

plan: Increase exposure to leveraged ETFs SOXL and SPAL.
Trim SPY to reduce concentration and fund new adds.
Add to CRWD and add NVDU and GUSH as strong setups.
This plan leans into high-conviction names and leverage.
Expect higher potential returns but also significantly larger drawdowns.
aggressive.read: This plan targets around 4% per month by concentrating on high-scoring setups and increasing leverage, including leveraged ETFs. Expect amplified gains and losses, with leveraged ETFs subject to daily decay. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage potentially large drawdowns.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs and high-beta single names.
Sell volatile sector exposure like SMH.
Increase QQQI for income and SPY for stability.
This plan prioritizes reliable monthly distributions.
Expect very low drawdowns and steady, albeit variable, income.
income.read: This plan targets around 1% per month by focusing on income-generating assets like QQQI and stable broad index ETFs like SPY. It cuts all leveraged and high-beta positions to minimize volatility. While aiming for steady distributions, NAV can still decline in market downturns.
